title: zerna pumpelliana subsp. vogulica
intro: Zerna pumpelliana subsp. vogulica, also known as Vogul's fescue or Siberian fescue, is a plant species within the Poaceae family. It is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and the Russian Far East, where it grows in well-drained soils and rocky areas. The medicinal properties of Zerna pumpelliana subsp. vogulica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and skin conditions. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are said to have various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved immune function.
